title: Origins of life on Earth
description: Test your knowledge of origins of life on Earth!

assessment_items.question_content:
Earth formed approximately 4.5 billion years ago. Until roughly 3.9 billion years ago, the conditions of early Earth were too hostile to support sustained life. The earliest strong evidence of life in the fossil record dates to 3.5 billion years ago.

**Based on the information above, which of the following describes when life most likely originated on Earth?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Over 4.5 billion years ago
Between 4.5 and 3.9 billion years ago
Between 3.9 and 3.5 billion years ago
Less than 3.5 billion years ago

assessment_items.question_content:
Earth formed approximately 4.5 billion years ago. Until roughly 3.9 billion years ago, the conditions of early Earth were too hostile to support sustained life. The earliest strong evidence of life in the fossil record dates to 3.5 billion years ago.

**Based on the information above, which of the following timelines highlights the time period when life most likely originated on Earth?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

The tip of the arrow pointing to the left is marked 4.5 and the arrow tip is pink.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/39c234b7a069179c182f4f6ef4504a1a.png)

There is a pink segment on the arrow from 3.9 to 3.5.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/5caf560e4edb2dae41317486334801f9.png)

The right arrow head beginning at 3.5 is pink.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/9e45c7c2198683848893488589f7510d.png)

assessment_items.question_content:
The following timeline shows the approximate timing of several important events in Earth’s history.

[[☃ image 1]]

**Based on the timeline above, which of the following describes when life most likely originated on Earth?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

options.alt: A two-directional arrow labeled Billions of years ago. The line between the tips of the arrow is marked with 17 evenly spaced vertical lines and moving from left to right, the lines are marked at 4.7, 4.5, 4.3, 4.1, 3.9, 3.7, 3.5, 3.3, and 3.1 and in between each labeled mark is an unmarked line. There is a blue dot at 4.5 with a label Earth forms; there is an orange dot at 3.9 with the label conditions on Earth begin to be able to support life; and there is a purple dot at 3.5 with the label First strong evidence of life in the fossil record.

choices.content: Over 4.5 billion years ago
Between 4.5 and 3.9 billion years ago
Between 3.9 and 3.5 billion years ago
Less than 3.5 billion years ago

assessment_items.question_content:
In 1953, the scientists Stanley Miller and Harold Urey conducted an experiment that provided support for the hypothesis that molecules important to life could have formed abiotically under Earth’s early conditions. The apparatus used in the experiment is pictured below.

[[☃ image 1]]

**Which biological molecules were generated during the Miller-Urey experiment?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

[[☃ explanation 1]]

options.explanation: [Miller and Urey's experiment](http://www.ck12.org/book/CK-12-Biology-Advanced-Concepts/section/10.8/)," by CK-12 Foundation, [CC BY-NC 3.0](http://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-nc/3.0/).

options.alt: An image of the apparatus used in the Miller and Urey experiment. There is a bunsen burner labeled heat source under a flask with water in it colored blue. The flask has  2 arms extending from it. The arm extending upwards towards the top  of the diagram has an arrow inside it pointing upwards and the label direction of circulation. Near the top of the arm is a tube connected to a valve with the label gas inlet. The upward extending arm makes a 90 degree bend to the left, continues across the top of the diagram and then bends 90 degrees downward to empty into a circular tank. The tank has a zigzag line in it with lines extending from either end and labeled electrodes. Above the zigzag line is the label spark. Inside the circular tank are the labels H 2 O, C H 4, N H 3 and H 2. From the circular tank a tube exits below it and passes through a structure labeled cooling. The tube then makes a 90 degree turn to the right and extends towards the right. Halfway across the bottom of the diagram, the tube makes a 90 degree bend downward and then continues towards the right before making a 90 degree bend upward and joining the second arm of the flask that is filled with water. In the region between where the tube bends downward and then upward the tube is filled with water.

choices.content: Only amino acids
Both amino acids and polypeptides
Only nucleotides
Both nucleotides and RNA polymers

assessment_items.question_content:
In 1953, the scientists Stanley Miller and Harold Urey conducted an experiment that provided support for the hypothesis that molecules important to life could have formed abiotically under Earth’s early conditions. The apparatus used in the experiment is pictured below, with certain parts labeled A-D.

[[☃ image 1]]

**Which of the following statements is true about the Miller-Urey experiment?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

[[☃ explanation 1]]

options.explanation: [Miller and Urey's experiment](http://www.ck12.org/book/CK-12-Biology-Advanced-Concepts/section/10.8/)," by CK-12 Foundation, [CC BY-NC 3.0](http://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-nc/3.0/).

options.alt: An image of the apparatus used in the Miller and Urey experiment. There is a bunsen burner labeled heat source under a flask with water in it colored blue. The flask has 2 arms extending from it. The arm extending upwards towards the top  of the diagram has an arrow inside it pointing upwards and the label direction of circulation. Near the top of the arm is a tube connected to a valve with the label gas inlet. The upward extending arm makes a 90 degree bend to the left, continues across the top of the diagram and then bends 90 degrees downward to empty into a circular tank. The tank has a zigzag line in it with lines extending from either end and labeled electrodes. Inside the circular tank are the labels H 2 O, C H 4, N H 3 and H 2. From the circular tank a tube exits below it and passes through a structure labeled cooling. The tube then makes a 90 degree turn to the right and extends towards the right. Halfway across the bottom of the diagram, the tube makes a 90 degree bend downward and then continues towards the right before making a 90 degree bend upward and joining the second arm of the flask that is filled with water. In the region between where the tube bends downward and then upward the tube is filled with water.

choices.content: During the experiment both RNA and DNA nucleotides were collected from the part of the apparatus labeled A.
At the end of the experiment, a variety of amino acids were found in the part of the apparatus labeled B.
At the end of the experiment, polypeptides with various sequences were found in the part of the apparatus labeled C.
During the experiment, RNA polymers were formed from the mixture of gases let into the apparatus at the part labeled D.

assessment_items.question_content:
In 1953, the scientists Stanley Miller and Harold Urey conducted an experiment that provided support for the hypothesis that molecules important to life could have formed abiotically under Earth’s early conditions. The apparatus used in the experiment is pictured below.

[[☃ image 1]]

**Which of the following statements is true about the Miller-Urey experiment?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

[[☃ explanation 1]]

options.explanation: [Miller and Urey's experiment](http://www.ck12.org/book/CK-12-Biology-Advanced-Concepts/section/10.8/)," by CK-12 Foundation, [CC BY-NC 3.0](http://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-nc/3.0/).

options.alt: An image of the apparatus used in the Miller and Urey experiment. There is a bunsen burner labeled heat source under a flask with water in it colored blue. The flask has  2 arms extending from it. The arm extending upwards towards the top  of the diagram has an arrow inside it pointing upwards and the label direction of circulation. Near the top of the arm is a tube connected to a valve with the label gas inlet. The upward extending arm makes a 90 degree bend to the left, continues across the top of the diagram and then bends 90 degrees downward to empty into a circular tank. The tank has a zigzag line in it with lines extending from either end and labeled electrodes. Inside the circular tank are the labels H 2 O, C H 4, N H 3 and H 2. From the circular tank a tube exits below it and passes through a structure labeled cooling. The tube then makes a 90 degree turn to the right and extends towards the right. Halfway across the bottom of the diagram, the tube makes a 90 degree bend downward and then continues towards the right before making a 90 degree bend upward and joining the second arm of the flask that is filled with water. In the region between where the tube bends downward and then upward the tube is filled with water.

choices.content: During the experiment, the scientists generated different types of carbohydrates by exposing a mixture of gases to light in a specialized apparatus.
During the experiment, the scientists generated different types of lipids by heating water together with certain chemicals in a specialized apparatus.
During the experiment, the scientists generated polypeptides with various sequences by exposing gases in a chamber to an electric spark.
During the experiment, the scientists generated a variety of amino acids by exposing gases in a chamber to an electric spark.

assessment_items.question_content:
One model of the origin of life on Earth hypothesizes that organic molecules arose abiotically under the atmospheric conditions of early Earth. Another model hypothesizes that these organic molecules arrived on Earth from space via a meteorite or other source.

**Which of the following questions related to the models described above is most scientifically testable?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Can organic molecules be generated abiotically under low temperature and high levels of UV radiation, similar to conditions in space?
Was the synthesis of simple organic molecules on early Earth a stepping stone toward the evolution of complex organic polymers?
Did simple organic molecules form abiotically in the atmosphere of early Earth with the goal of generating life?
Why do organic molecules exist both on Earth and on meteorites that have come to Earth from space?

assessment_items.question_content:
According to the Oparin-Haldane hypothesis, simple inorganic molecules underwent chemical reactions and formed organic molecules under the conditions of early Earth. These organic molecules gradually became more complex, eventually giving rise to metabolic networks and living cells.

**Which of the following questions related to the Oparin-Haldane hypothesis is most scientifically testable?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Can organic molecules be generated abiotically in a chamber that mimics the conditions of early Earth?
How many types of organic molecules were present during the time that metabolic networks began to form?
Did metabolic networks and living cells form to make the Earth a more interesting place?
Why are there two main types of living cells (prokaryotic and eukaryotic cells) in existence today?

assessment_items.question_content:
One model of the origin of life on Earth hypothesizes that organic molecules arose abiotically from inorganic precursors under certain conditions on early Earth. Some scientists speculate that this could have occurred near the openings of volcanoes. Others think it likely occurred near deep-sea alkaline vents, which release warm, alkaline water.

**Which of the following questions related to the information above is most scientifically testable?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Can organic molecules be generated abiotically in a warm, high-pH environment, similar to that near an alkaline vent?
Were the first organic molecules formed near the openings of volcanoes, or near deep-sea vents?
Did life originate as a way to make Earth a superior planet?
Why are atmospheric conditions near the openings of volcanoes different from those further away from volcanoes?

assessment_items.question_content:
The RNA World Hypothesis proposes that RNA was the earliest self-replicating biological molecule, performing functions in early cells that are now fulfilled by other biological molecules.

One piece of evidence in support of the RNA World Hypothesis is that RNA molecules can encode genetic information. This provides support for the idea that, in early cells, RNA carried out a function similar to that of DNA.

**Which of the following is an additional piece of evidence in support of the RNA World Hypothesis?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Like proteins, RNA can carry out catalytic activities.
Like lipids, RNA can help make up the structure of a cell membrane.
Like chlorophylls, RNA can capture light energy for photosynthesis.
Like carbohydrates, RNA can provide physical support within cell walls.

assessment_items.question_content:
The RNA World Hypothesis proposes that RNA was the earliest self-replicating biological molecule, performing functions in early cells that are now fulfilled by other biological molecules.

One piece of evidence in support of the RNA World Hypothesis is that RNA molecules can catalyze chemical reactions. This provides support for the idea that, in early cells, RNA carried out a function similar to that of proteins.

**Which of the following is an additional piece of evidence in support of the RNA World Hypothesis?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Like DNA, RNA can encode genetic information.
Like phospholipids, RNA can form a bilayer in an aqueous environment.
Like cellulose, RNA can provide physical support within cell walls.
Like chlorophylls, RNA can capture light energy for photosynthesis.

assessment_items.question_content:
The RNA World Hypothesis proposes that RNA was the earliest self-replicating biological molecule, performing functions in early cells that are now fulfilled by other biological molecules.

**Which two of the following pieces of evidence support the RNA World Hypothesis?**

👁️ **Note that there are 2 answers to this question.**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Like DNA, RNA can encode genetic information.
Like proteins, RNA can carry out catalytic activities.
Like phospholipids, RNA can form a bilayer in an aqueous environment.
Like carbohydrates, RNA can provide physical support within cell walls.
